Shishir Agrawal
  • Mathematics
  • Teaching
  • CV
  • महफ़िल · محفل

Overview

Broadly speaking, I study algebraic geometry. More specifically, here are some mathematical words and phrases that I find exciting/intriguing:

\(p\)-adic, nonarchimedean, adic space, perfectoid, pro-étale, overconvergent, (bounded) Robba ring, (arithmetic) D-module, rigid cohomology, de Rham(-Witt), crystalline, cotangent complex, (higher) category, derived, homological, topos, derivator, non-noetherian, locally analytic representation, local Langlands correspondences, …

I completed my PhD at UC Berkeley in 2018. My advisor was David Nadler. My PhD thesis is available here. There are a few typos in this document, but they’re not terribly serious. The arXiv and published versions below correct these typos, but they omit some of the expository content of my thesis.

Papers

All papers at arXiv.org/a/agrawal_s_1.

  • From category \(\mathcal{O}^\infty\) to locally analytic representations. With Matthias Strauch. arXiv:2011.12370. Submitted.
  • Deformations of overconvergent isocrystals on the projective line. arXiv:1710.03404. doi:10.1016/j.jnt.2019.11.013.

Talks

Here are some slides and/or notes for some of the talks I’ve given.

  • From category \(\mathcal{O}^\infty\) to locally analytic representations. I’ve given a couple of similar but not completely identical talks about the paper of this name.
    • Rennes, Cornell (link forthcoming). May 2021 at the Algebraic Geometry Seminar at Cornell University, and then a bit later in May 2021 at the Séminare de géométrie arithmétique at the Institut de recherche mathématique de Rennes.
    • Padova. April 2021 at the p-adic representations and arithmetic D-modules seminar, organized by Andrés Sarrazola Alzate and Francesco Baldassari, at the Università degli Studi di Padova. This talk assumes some background material that had been discussed in preceding seminar talks.
  • Perspective: The Mathematics of Depicting Depth. Slides for a talk I gave at Colorado College’s Fearless Fridays seminar in February 2020.
  • A Smörgåsbord of Arithmetic Geometry. Slides for a talk I gave at Colorado College’s Fearless Fridays seminar in February 2018.
  • Contangent complex. Some (incomplete!) notes I compiled when I was preparing for a talk I gave in David Nadler’s fall 2013 Geometric Representation Theory seminar.

If you find errors in any of these, please let me know!

Miscellaneous

Here are some miscellaneous mathematical writings.

  • Off on a tangent. Some notes about derivatives.
  • Exponential at infinity. A fun explicit calculation I did during summer 2017 involving an irregular rank 1 overconvergent differential module over the Robba ring.
  • Fundamental group of a curve. An expository term paper from Martin Olsson’s Spring 2012 Algebraic Geometry course.

If you find errors in any of these, please let me know!
